task_completion.description: Verify the anchor point is set to OAK.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text input field or selection for the anchor/starting waypoint in the SAR tool contains 'OAK'.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Parallel Track' is selected as the SAR pattern type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, radio button, or menu selection shows 'Parallel Track' (or 'Parallel') as the active search pattern type.

task_completion.description: Verify the specific SAR parameters (Heading, Length, Spacing, Legs) are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Input fields for Heading/Direction, Leg Length, Track Spacing, and Number of Legs contain '360', '10', '1', and '6' respectively.

task_completion.description: Verify the SAR pattern is loaded into the active flight plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app's flight plan or route list screen shows a sequence of generated waypoints (e.g., SAR01, SAR02, or a list of coordinates) indicating the pattern was created and activated.

task_completion.description: Verify the final flight plan is visible on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view is active and displays a parallel track search pattern (a series of 6 parallel lines connected by short turns) originating near OAK (San Francisco Bay Area).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Avare application GUI to create the plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Avare app interface being used, not a terminal, script editor, or SQLite database viewer injecting the database directly.

integrity.description: Verify the map shown is the actual Avare navigation map.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map includes standard Avare UI elements (like GPS status, menu buttons at the bottom/corners) and is not a web browser showing a generic map or image search.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually entered the parameters into the SAR tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SAR dialog/menu with the specific parameters typed in, proving the agent didn't just load a pre-saved flight plan file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is Avare (com.ds.avare). 'OAK' refers to the Oakland VOR, located in the San Francisco Bay Area. Heading 360 represents North. A 'Parallel Track' SAR pattern with 6 legs, 10 NM length, and 1 NM spacing will visually appear on the map as a zig-zag grid of 6 parallel lines oriented North, spaced closely together. The flight plan data is stored internally in Avare's SQLite database, but the agent must use the GUI to generate it.
